mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in industrial production signals improving real output and near-term earnings momentum; higher INDPRO growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 112.0991 as of May 2006
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO growth is followed by non-positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or Rank IC below 0.03 in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4.5% after a tightening sequence, equity discount rates and financing costs rise enough to compress valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% or remains above 4.5% after a 3-month increase
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 4.94% as of May 2006
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth combined with unemployment at or below 5% indicates household demand is still supported by labor income, raising expected revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE is at or below 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 364198.0 and UNRATE was 4.6 as of May 2006
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-growth and low-UNRATE state has a 60-day forward win rate below 50% or does not outperform periods lacking the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: A material downturn in housing starts leads residential investment and credit-sensitive consumption lower, signaling weaker cyclical demand and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average or falls more than 10% year over year
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 1849.0 as of April 2006
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or by no deterioration in GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices during a period of high federal funds rates signals inflation persistence and further policy restraint, reducing risk appetite and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 201.9 and FEDFUNDS was 4.94 as of May 2006
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-inflation and high-FEDFUNDS state has non-negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or fails to show lower returns than high-FEDFUNDS-only states.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment from a low base marks a transition from tight labor conditions to weakening income growth, increasing recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months after being below 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 4.6 as of May 2006
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rollover triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered mean returns are not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth reflects broad demand expansion that supports aggregate corporate cash flows; higher GDPC1 growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 11394.7 as of 2006 Q1 information available by the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth produces a 60-day S&P 500 Rank IC below 0.03 in walk-forward tests or does not outperform negative-growth observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment with unemployment below 5% during high policy rates signals wage-pressure risk rather than early-cycle recovery, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive, UNRATE is below 5.0%, and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 135106.0, UNRATE was 4.6, and FEDFUNDS was 4.94 as of May 2006
failure_condition: Reject if this joint tight-labor and high-rate state has positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if its return distribution is not lower than payroll-growth-only observations.
